🔒
Chapter Two Is Sealed
The same key that opened Chapter One opens this too
Chapter Two A Continuation Of My Heart
What I Never Fully Said
A quiet letter to my Niku
In My Own Words

Niku, Chapter One held the invoice of my heart — playful, dressed up, easy to smile at. This chapter is quieter. This is the part I usually only feel, the part that comes out when I'm alone with my thoughts and your name, so let me finally write it down.

After we first met, I kept counting the times after that — three more meetings, three small chances the world gave us to be in the same place at the same time. I don't remember them as dates on a calendar. I remember them as a handful of minutes that felt longer than they were, minutes where nothing about the day's stress could reach me, because you were right there.

Something as small as your hand near mine, or standing close enough that I could stop performing and just be — I never asked for anything more than that closeness, and I never will need to. It wasn't about wanting something. It was about how safe I felt in it, how much I wanted to hold on to that feeling and never let it go.

That's the simplest way I can put it — being near you is the calmest I know how to feel.

I remember the rain. I remember the bike, and I remember the scooty rides after — ordinary rides that somehow became some of my favorite memories, because you sat behind me and the whole city disappeared.

And then I remember dropping you back to your hostel. That gate, that last turn where I'd watch you walk in. Every single time, before I even reached the end of the road, something in my chest would already start missing you. I don't know how to explain it except to say: the length of your absence and the size of the ache in me are exactly the same.

With you, I forget things I didn't think I could forget — the tension of my career, the weight I carry through an ordinary week, all of it goes quiet. I'm not saying this to sound poetic. I'm saying it because it's true, and because I don't say it to you enough out loud.

A Promise I Made Quietly

We visited the Vishnu–Lakshmi temple together one day, and I don't think either of us said out loud how much that moment mattered to me. Standing there, I made a quiet promise to myself — not the kind spoken for show, but the kind you mean.

I know it's early. I know a few years stand between where we are now and where I hope we'll be. But I'm telling you plainly, without any exaggeration: I want to marry you, in time, the right way, with our families and with respect. This isn't a rushed word — it's a direction I've already chosen.

The Promise We Made
We will not let anything break this bond, in any situation.
We will stay together, we will keep choosing each other, and we will keep talking — always.
We will grow toward our success and toward our love, side by side, not apart.
Right Now

I'm scared sometimes — scared of losing you, scared of a version of my life where I have to learn to live with your absence instead of your presence. I don't want to get used to missing you. I want your presence in every possible way, not just on a screen, not just in a call, but really, physically, here.

Video calls carry your voice to me, and I'm grateful for that every single day. But they can't carry your hand, or the quiet of just sitting next to you. That's the piece I'm still waiting for, and the piece I'll keep working toward.

This is all real. This is all genuine — every word of it, written while my eyes were a little full, because that's what happens when I try to put you into sentences. I love you, Niku. That's the whole of it.

Moments We Made
That laugh I keep replaying
You, and all that green behind you
The flowers that rode in the back seat
Just sitting close, no reason needed
Lying in the grass, half-listening to the sky
The rain, and you laughing through it
Still raining, still us
Ducking under the same umbrella of luck
Vibes, exactly
Us, unfiltered
Yours, always
Written With A Full Heart
From Your Gugu (Divyaa)
Chapter Two  ·  For My Niku  ·  Written With Love
If there are spelling mistakes here, forgive them — every feeling above is exactly, genuinely mine.
